milestones.title: TCP Transport
milestones.vision: Add TCP socket transport to wx-cli's daemon communication layer with trait-based abstraction, enabling remote clients to query WeChat data over the network.

key_risks.risk: Windows named pipe refactoring may require interprocess crate API changes
key_risks.whyItMatters: interprocess crate API differs from std Unix sockets, may need adaptation for trait compatibility

key_risks.risk: Daemon start subcommand conflicts with existing auto-start behavior
key_risks.whyItMatters: Currently daemon auto-starts on first query via ensure_daemon(). New explicit start must coexist.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Windows named pipe trait compatibility with interprocess crate
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Transport traits work for Unix socket and named pipe

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: TCP bind and accept behavior on all platforms
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: TCP listener accepts connections and handles requests correctly

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Protocol works identically over TCP as over local transport
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: End-to-end TCP communication returns correct results

milestones.verification_contract:
- Contract verification: `cargo check` on all three targets, unit tests for protocol handling
- Integration verification: Manual smoke test of CLI ↔ daemon over TCP on localhost
- Operational verification: Daemon starts/stops cleanly with TCP, handles bind errors
- UAT / human verification: Verify TCP results match local transport results
milestones.verification_integration: Manual smoke test: daemon on TCP + client queries return same data as local transport
milestones.verification_operational: Daemon starts with --tcp, binds to specified address, handles errors cleanly. Client connects via TCP, fails clearly on unreachable address.
definition_of_done: Transport abstraction layer wired into daemon and client
cargo check passes on macOS, Linux, and Windows targets
Daemon can listen on local + TCP simultaneously
Client connects via TCP with --tcp, errors clearly on failure
Manual smoke test passes: CLI ↔ daemon over TCP on localhost
milestones.requirement_coverage:
Covers: R001, R002, R003, R004, R005, R006, R007, R008
Partially covers: none
Leaves for later: R020 (TLS), R021 (Auth), R022 (Keepalive)
Orphan risks: none

slices.title: Transport abstraction layer
slices.demo: Refactor complete, `cargo check` passes on all platforms, existing behavior unchanged. Transport traits defined and implemented for Unix socket + Windows named pipe.
slices.goal: Refactor transport layer into trait-based abstraction, eliminating platform-specific duplication. Implement Unix socket and Windows named pipe using new traits. Add `wx daemon start` subcommand.
slices.success_criteria:
- `cargo check` passes on all three target platforms
- Existing CLI commands work unchanged (no regression)
- Transport traits (Listener, Connector) defined in `src/transport/traits.rs`
- Protocol handling shared in `src/transport/protocol.rs`
- `wx daemon start` subcommand exists and starts daemon
slices.integration_closure: Daemon starts via `wx daemon start` and listens on local transport (Unix socket / named pipe). Client queries work via local transport as before.
slices.observability_impact: Daemon startup logs show which transports are active

slices.title: TCP server support
slices.demo: `wx daemon start --tcp 127.0.0.1:9876` starts daemon listening on TCP port 9876
slices.goal: Implement TCP server support. Add `--tcp` flag to daemon start. Daemon listens on local transport AND TCP simultaneously when --tcp is specified.
slices.success_criteria:
- `wx daemon start --tcp 127.0.0.1:9876` starts daemon on both local and TCP
- TCP connections accepted and handled correctly
- TCP bind failure produces clear error message
- `cargo check` passes on all platforms
slices.integration_closure: Daemon accepts TCP connections. JSON-line protocol works over TCP. Bind errors are clear.
slices.observability_impact: Daemon logs show TCP bind address and accepted connections

slices.title: TCP client + global --tcp flag
slices.demo: `wx sessions --tcp 127.0.0.1:9876` connects via TCP and returns session data
slices.goal: Implement TCP client support. Add global `--tcp host:port` flag to CLI. Client connects directly via TCP when flag is specified, with no local fallback.
slices.success_criteria:
- `wx sessions --tcp 127.0.0.1:9876` connects via TCP and returns results
- `wx sessions --tcp 127.0.0.1:9999` fails with clear error within 15s
- `wx daemon status --tcp 127.0.0.1:9876` works over TCP
- `wx daemon logs --tcp 127.0.0.1:9876` works over TCP
- All commands without --tcp still work via local transport
slices.integration_closure: Client connects via TCP to running daemon. All query commands work. Error messages clear on failure.
slices.observability_impact: Client error messages show TCP address when connection fails

slices.title: Integration smoke test
slices.demo: Daemon on TCP + client queries return same data as local transport
slices.goal: End-to-end integration verification. Daemon on TCP, client queries over TCP, results match local transport.
slices.success_criteria:
- Manual smoke test: daemon started with --tcp, client queries over TCP
- TCP and local transport return identical results
- No regression to local-only mode
- All cargo check targets pass
slices.integration_closure: Full end-to-end: CLI ↔ daemon over TCP on localhost, same results as local transport.
slices.observability_impact: Observable end-to-end TCP communication between CLI and daemon
